import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")

try:
    experiment_data = np.load(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), allow_pickle=True
    ).item()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading experiment data: {e}")
    experiment_data = None

if experiment_data is not None:
    # Plot 1: Training and Validation Loss Curves
    try:
        plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
        epochs = experiment_data["training"]["epochs"]
        train_loss = experiment_data["training"]["losses"]["train"]
        val_loss = experiment_data["training"]["losses"]["val"]
        plt.plot(epochs, train_loss, "b-", label="Train Loss", linewidth=2)
        plt.plot(epochs, val_loss, "r-", label="Validation Loss", linewidth=2)
        plt.xlabel("Epoch")
        plt.ylabel("MSE Loss")
        plt.title(
            "Warehouse Robot Task Allocation\nCongestion Predictor Training/Validation Loss"
        )
        plt.legend()
        plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "warehouse_training_loss_curves.png"), dpi=150
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating training loss plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 2: Throughput vs max_steps Comparison
    try:
        plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
        summary = experiment_data["summary"]
        x = np.array(summary["max_steps"])
        plt.plot(
            x,
            summary["avg_dist"],
            "bo-",
            label="Distance-based",
            markersize=8,
            linewidth=2,
        )
        plt.plot(
            x,
            summary["avg_cata"],
            "gs-",
            label="Congestion-aware (CATA)",
            markersize=8,
            linewidth=2,
        )
        plt.xlabel("max_steps (Simulation Length)")
        plt.ylabel("Average Throughput (tasks/min)")
        plt.title(
            "Warehouse Robot Task Allocation\nThroughput Comparison: Distance-based vs Congestion-aware"
        )
        plt.legend()
        plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        plt.xticks(x)
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "warehouse_throughput_vs_maxsteps.png"), dpi=150
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating throughput comparison plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 3: Improvement Percentage Bar Chart
    try:
        plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
        summary = experiment_data["summary"]
        x = summary["max_steps"]
        improvements = summary["avg_improvement"]
        colors = ["green" if v >= 0 else "red" for v in improvements]
        plt.bar(x, improvements, color=colors, alpha=0.7, width=40)
        plt.axhline(y=0, color="black", linestyle="--", linewidth=1)
        plt.xlabel("max_steps (Simulation Length)")
        plt.ylabel("Average Improvement (%)")
        plt.title(
            "Warehouse Robot Task Allocation\nCATA Improvement over Distance-based Method by max_steps"
        )
        plt.xticks(x)
        plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3, axis="y")
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "warehouse_improvement_by_maxsteps.png"), dpi=150
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating improvement bar chart: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 4: Throughput by Agent Count for Different max_steps
    try:
        plt.figure(figsize=(12, 7))
        max_steps_values = [100, 200, 300, 400, 500]
        colors = plt.cm.viridis(np.linspace(0, 1, len(max_steps_values)))
        for idx, max_steps in enumerate(max_steps_values):
            key = f"max_steps_{max_steps}"
            agent_counts = experiment_data["hyperparam_tuning"][key]["n_agents"]
            cata_throughput = experiment_data["hyperparam_tuning"][key][
                "congestion_aware"
            ]["throughput"]
            plt.plot(
                agent_counts,
                cata_throughput,
                "o-",
                color=colors[idx],
                label=f"max_steps={max_steps}",
                markersize=8,
                linewidth=2,
            )
        plt.xlabel("Number of Agents")
        plt.ylabel("Throughput (tasks/min)")
        plt.title(
            "Warehouse Robot Task Allocation\nCATA Throughput by Agent Count Across Different max_steps"
        )
        plt.legend(title="Simulation Length")
        plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "warehouse_cata_throughput_by_agents.png"),
            dpi=150,
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating throughput by agent count plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 5: Heatmap of Improvement across max_steps and agent counts
    try:
        plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))
        max_steps_values = [100, 200, 300, 400, 500]
        agent_counts = experiment_data["hyperparam_tuning"]["max_steps_100"]["n_agents"]
        improvement_matrix = []
        for max_steps in max_steps_values:
            key = f"max_steps_{max_steps}"
            improvement_matrix.append(
                experiment_data["hyperparam_tuning"][key]["improvement"]
            )
        improvement_matrix = np.array(improvement_matrix)
        im = plt.imshow(improvement_matrix, cmap="RdYlGn", aspect="auto")
        plt.colorbar(im, label="Improvement (%)")
        plt.xticks(range(len(agent_counts)), agent_counts)
        plt.yticks(range(len(max_steps_values)), max_steps_values)
        plt.xlabel("Number of Agents")
        plt.ylabel("max_steps")
        plt.title(
            "Warehouse Robot Task Allocation\nCATA Improvement (%) Heatmap: max_steps vs Agent Count"
        )
        for i in range(len(max_steps_values)):
            for j in range(len(agent_counts)):
                plt.text(
                    j,
                    i,
                    f"{improvement_matrix[i, j]:.1f}%",
                    ha="center",
                    va="center",
                    fontsize=9,
                )
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "warehouse_improvement_heatmap.png"), dpi=150
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating improvement heatmap: {e}")
        plt.close()

print("All plots saved to working directory.")

import os
import numpy as np

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")
experiment_data = np.load(
    os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), allow_pickle=True
).item()

# Training metrics
print("=" * 60)
print("DATASET: Training (Congestion Predictor)")
print("=" * 60)
train_losses = experiment_data["training"]["losses"]["train"]
val_losses = experiment_data["training"]["losses"]["val"]
final_epoch = experiment_data["training"]["epochs"][-1]

print(f"Final Training Loss (Epoch {final_epoch}): {train_losses[-1]:.6f}")
print(f"Final Validation Loss (Epoch {final_epoch}): {val_losses[-1]:.6f}")
print(f"Best Training Loss: {min(train_losses):.6f}")
print(f"Best Validation Loss: {min(val_losses):.6f}")

# Hyperparameter tuning metrics
print("\n" + "=" * 60)
print("DATASET: Hyperparameter Tuning (max_steps)")
print("=" * 60)

for key in experiment_data["hyperparam_tuning"]:
    tuning_data = experiment_data["hyperparam_tuning"][key]
    max_steps_value = key.replace("max_steps_", "")

    print(f"\n--- Configuration: max_steps = {max_steps_value} ---")

    dist_throughputs = tuning_data["distance_based"]["throughput"]
    dist_stds = tuning_data["distance_based"]["std"]
    cata_throughputs = tuning_data["congestion_aware"]["throughput"]
    cata_stds = tuning_data["congestion_aware"]["std"]
    improvements = tuning_data["improvement"]
    n_agents = tuning_data["n_agents"]

    # Best distance-based throughput
    best_dist_idx = np.argmax(dist_throughputs)
    print(
        f"Best Distance-Based Throughput (tasks/min): {dist_throughputs[best_dist_idx]:.2f} ± {dist_stds[best_dist_idx]:.2f} (n_agents={n_agents[best_dist_idx]})"
    )

    # Best congestion-aware throughput
    best_cata_idx = np.argmax(cata_throughputs)
    print(
        f"Best Congestion-Aware Throughput (tasks/min): {cata_throughputs[best_cata_idx]:.2f} ± {cata_stds[best_cata_idx]:.2f} (n_agents={n_agents[best_cata_idx]})"
    )

    # Best improvement
    best_imp_idx = np.argmax(improvements)
    print(
        f"Best CATA Improvement over Distance-Based (%): {improvements[best_imp_idx]:.2f}% (n_agents={n_agents[best_imp_idx]})"
    )

    # Average metrics across agent counts
    print(
        f"Average Distance-Based Throughput (tasks/min): {np.mean(dist_throughputs):.2f}"
    )
    print(
        f"Average Congestion-Aware Throughput (tasks/min): {np.mean(cata_throughputs):.2f}"
    )
    print(f"Average Improvement (%): {np.mean(improvements):.2f}%")

# Summary metrics
print("\n" + "=" * 60)
print("DATASET: Summary (Across All max_steps Configurations)")
print("=" * 60)

summary = experiment_data["summary"]
max_steps_list = summary["max_steps"]
avg_improvements = summary["avg_improvement"]
avg_dist_throughputs = summary["avg_dist"]
avg_cata_throughputs = summary["avg_cata"]

# Best overall configuration
best_config_idx = np.argmax(avg_improvements)
print(
    f"Best max_steps Configuration for Improvement: {max_steps_list[best_config_idx]}"
)
print(f"Best Average Improvement (%): {avg_improvements[best_config_idx]:.2f}%")
print(
    f"Best Configuration Average Distance-Based Throughput (tasks/min): {avg_dist_throughputs[best_config_idx]:.2f}"
)
print(
    f"Best Configuration Average Congestion-Aware Throughput (tasks/min): {avg_cata_throughputs[best_config_idx]:.2f}"
)

# Highest throughput configuration
best_throughput_idx = np.argmax(avg_cata_throughputs)
print(
    f"\nBest max_steps Configuration for CATA Throughput: {max_steps_list[best_throughput_idx]}"
)
print(
    f"Highest Average Congestion-Aware Throughput (tasks/min): {avg_cata_throughputs[best_throughput_idx]:.2f}"
)

# Overall statistics
print(
    f"\nOverall Average Distance-Based Throughput (tasks/min): {np.mean(avg_dist_throughputs):.2f}"
)
print(
    f"Overall Average Congestion-Aware Throughput (tasks/min): {np.mean(avg_cata_throughputs):.2f}"
)
print(f"Overall Average Improvement (%): {np.mean(avg_improvements):.2f}%")

Congestion-aware throughput in tasks per minute. For each max_steps configuration, the best value is the reported best throughput and the final value is the reported average throughput. Summary rows include the highest average configuration, best-improvement configuration average, and the overall average.

For each max_steps configuration, the best value is the reported best improvement and the final value is the reported average improvement. plot_analyses.analysis: The congestion predictor training is stable and converges quickly. Train loss drops from about 0.068 to about 0.032 immediately, then both train and validation losses fall sharply around epochs 6-8 and gradually flatten near 0.0075-0.008 by the end. The train and validation curves almost overlap after the early phase, which is strong evidence that the current architecture is not overfitting and that generalization is good under this setup. The main implication for baseline tuning is that the model likely does not need many more than about 15-20 epochs to reach most of its achievable performance, since improvements after that point are marginal. This suggests testing earlier stopping, a slightly larger learning rate warmup/decay schedule, or a reduced epoch budget to cut training cost without harming final predictive quality.

CATA throughput increases consistently with agent count for all tested simulation lengths, indicating that the method continues to provide usable allocation benefits as the environment becomes more populated. The strongest curve is max_steps=100, especially at 40 and 50 agents, where it clearly exceeds the longer-horizon settings. At 20 agents, max_steps=200 is slightly best, but this advantage disappears as density rises. The spread between curves grows at 50 agents, implying that simulation length choice matters more in heavier traffic. A key insight is that shorter rollout horizons appear to align better with the useful congestion prediction horizon in this first attempt, while longer max_steps may introduce delayed dynamics that dilute the allocation signal. For tuning, max_steps around 100-200 currently looks most promising, and evaluation should emphasize dense regimes because that is where differences become meaningful.

The improvement heatmap shows that gains are positive in most configurations but are highly non-uniform. The best results occur at max_steps=200 with 20 agents and 30 agents, reaching 7.6% and 6.7%, respectively. Performance becomes much weaker at higher agent counts, and there is even a negative result at max_steps=200 with 50 agents (-2.3%). Max_steps=100 is modest but consistently positive across all agent counts, while max_steps=400 gives the most balanced positive profile without catastrophic failure. This pattern suggests that the current congestion-aware cost is beneficial but somewhat brittle: certain horizon-task density combinations help the allocator avoid bottlenecks very effectively, while others may misprice congestion and hurt assignment quality. For a paper-quality story, this means the method currently shows promise but needs more robust tuning before claiming universally strong performance. The inconsistency at 50 agents is especially important because the hypothesis is strongest in dense settings.

The training subplot again confirms rapid loss convergence and near-identical train/validation behavior, so the predictor itself appears well-behaved. The throughput comparison at n_episodes=10 shows CATA outperforming the distance-based baseline at every agent count, with the largest absolute gap around 30 agents and smaller but still positive gains at 40 and 50 agents. The error bars indicate some variance, but the mean ordering remains favorable to CATA. The stability subplot comparing standard deviation across n_episodes suggests that n_episodes=10 provides the lowest average variability overall, which is also summarized in the stability score chart: about 6.55 for 10 episodes versus 7.11 for 5 and 7.34 for 20. This is useful experimentally because it implies that using 10 evaluation episodes gives the most reliable estimate among those tested, possibly because 5 is too noisy and 20 may include more heterogeneous rollout conditions. For baseline tuning, n_episodes=10 looks like the best evaluation protocol to report going forward.

Average improvement over the distance-based method is positive for every tested max_steps value, but the magnitude is modest. Max_steps=200 is best at about 3.45%, followed by max_steps=400 at about 2.84%. Max_steps=100, 300, and 500 cluster in the roughly 1.8-2.1% range. This supports the view that CATA is helping on average, but not yet at the level suggested by the longer-term research hypothesis. The strong result at 200 steps combined with weaker results elsewhere implies the method is sensitive to rollout horizon and that hyperparameter alignment between predictor horizon and environment horizon is likely important. A useful next step is to focus hyperparameter sweeps around the 200-step setting rather than spreading effort uniformly across all max_steps values.

Across all max_steps values, congestion-aware allocation has higher average throughput than distance-based allocation. The absolute margin is largest at 200 steps and remains clearly positive at 100, 300, 400, and 500. Neither method is monotonic in max_steps, but CATA stays consistently above the baseline, indicating that the learned congestion signal is adding value rather than only working in a single cherry-picked condition. The fact that the baseline drops sharply from 100 to 200 while CATA drops much less suggests that congestion-aware assignment is especially useful when simulation conditions become less favorable. This figure strengthens the main claim more than the heatmap alone because it emphasizes consistent dominance in mean throughput, even if the percentage gains are moderate.

This composite summary is internally consistent with the previous figures. The loss panel again shows a well-trained predictor with little sign of overfitting and diminishing returns after early epochs. The throughput-vs-max_steps panel confirms that CATA is always above the distance-based baseline, with the largest gain at 200 and a smaller but steady advantage elsewhere. The average-improvement panel reinforces that gains are positive but modest, peaking around 3.5%. The by-agent-count panel shows that throughput scales with agent count under all max_steps settings and that max_steps=100 is strongest at high agent counts, while 200 can be competitive at lower counts. Taken together, the overall picture is: the predictor trains successfully, CATA improves throughput over the baseline in aggregate, but performance is horizon-sensitive and the largest benefits are not yet concentrated in the densest regimes where the paper hypothesis would be most compelling. This argues for additional tuning of learning rate, batch size, and training duration around the best-performing rollout settings, along with more stress tests in high-density scenarios.

nodes.vlm_feedback_summary: Plots were received and are meaningful. They show a well-converged congestion predictor with minimal train/validation gap, consistent average throughput gains of CATA over distance-based allocation, and substantial sensitivity to max_steps and evaluation setup. Best average gains appear around max_steps=200, while max_steps=100 looks strongest at high agent counts. Results are promising but not yet uniformly strong in the densest settings, so further baseline-stage hyperparameter tuning and more robust cross-scenario evaluation are needed.

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")
os.makedirs(working_dir, exist_ok=True)

# Load all experiment data
experiment_data_path_list = [
    "experiments/2026-04-05_07-51-40_congestion_heatmap_allocation_attempt_0/logs/0-run/experiment_results/experiment_87616954740d4edf8d3bfc1fd571290b_proc_418/experiment_data.npy",
    "experiments/2026-04-05_07-51-40_congestion_heatmap_allocation_attempt_0/logs/0-run/experiment_results/experiment_c9cc50aa82bc4a36a7bcf8f367f4ec9c_proc_416/experiment_data.npy",
]

all_experiment_data = []
try:
    for experiment_data_path in experiment_data_path_list:
        if experiment_data_path is None or "None" in experiment_data_path:
            continue
        full_path = os.path.join(
            os.getenv("AI_SCIENTIST_ROOT", ""), experiment_data_path
        )
        if os.path.exists(full_path):
            experiment_data = np.load(full_path, allow_pickle=True).item()
            all_experiment_data.append(experiment_data)
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading experiment data: {e}")

print(f"Loaded {len(all_experiment_data)} experiment runs")

if len(all_experiment_data) > 0:
    # Plot 1: Aggregated Training and Validation Loss Curves with Standard Error
    try:
        plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
        all_train_losses = []
        all_val_losses = []
        epochs = None
        for exp_data in all_experiment_data:
            if epochs is None:
                epochs = np.array(exp_data["training"]["epochs"])
            all_train_losses.append(exp_data["training"]["losses"]["train"])
            all_val_losses.append(exp_data["training"]["losses"]["val"])

        all_train_losses = np.array(all_train_losses)
        all_val_losses = np.array(all_val_losses)

        train_mean = np.mean(all_train_losses, axis=0)
        train_se = np.std(all_train_losses, axis=0) / np.sqrt(len(all_experiment_data))
        val_mean = np.mean(all_val_losses, axis=0)
        val_se = np.std(all_val_losses, axis=0) / np.sqrt(len(all_experiment_data))

        plt.plot(epochs, train_mean, "b-", label="Train Loss (Mean)", linewidth=2)
        plt.fill_between(
            epochs,
            train_mean - train_se,
            train_mean + train_se,
            alpha=0.3,
            color="blue",
            label="Train SE",
        )
        plt.plot(epochs, val_mean, "r-", label="Validation Loss (Mean)", linewidth=2)
        plt.fill_between(
            epochs,
            val_mean - val_se,
            val_mean + val_se,
            alpha=0.3,
            color="red",
            label="Val SE",
        )
        plt.xlabel("Epoch")
        plt.ylabel("MSE Loss")
        plt.title(
            f"Warehouse Robot Task Allocation\nAggregated Training/Validation Loss (n={len(all_experiment_data)} runs)"
        )
        plt.legend()
        plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "warehouse_aggregated_training_loss.png"), dpi=150
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating aggregated training loss plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 2: Aggregated Throughput vs max_steps with Standard Error Bars
    try:
        plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
        all_avg_dist = []
        all_avg_cata = []
        max_steps = None
        for exp_data in all_experiment_data:
            if max_steps is None:
                max_steps = np.array(exp_data["summary"]["max_steps"])
            all_avg_dist.append(exp_data["summary"]["avg_dist"])
            all_avg_cata.append(exp_data["summary"]["avg_cata"])

        all_avg_dist = np.array(all_avg_dist)
        all_avg_cata = np.array(all_avg_cata)

        dist_mean = np.mean(all_avg_dist, axis=0)
        dist_se = np.std(all_avg_dist, axis=0) / np.sqrt(len(all_experiment_data))
        cata_mean = np.mean(all_avg_cata, axis=0)
        cata_se = np.std(all_avg_cata, axis=0) / np.sqrt(len(all_experiment_data))

        plt.errorbar(
            max_steps,
            dist_mean,
            yerr=dist_se,
            fmt="bo-",
            label="Distance-based (Mean ± SE)",
            markersize=8,
            linewidth=2,
            capsize=5,
        )
        plt.errorbar(
            max_steps,
            cata_mean,
            yerr=cata_se,
            fmt="gs-",
            label="CATA (Mean ± SE)",
            markersize=8,
            linewidth=2,
            capsize=5,
        )
        plt.xlabel("max_steps (Simulation Length)")
        plt.ylabel("Average Throughput (tasks/min)")
        plt.title(
            f"Warehouse Robot Task Allocation\nAggregated Throughput Comparison (n={len(all_experiment_data)} runs)"
        )
        plt.legend()
        plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        plt.xticks(max_steps)
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(
                working_dir, "warehouse_aggregated_throughput_vs_maxsteps.png"
            ),
            dpi=150,
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating aggregated throughput plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 3: Aggregated Improvement Bar Chart with Standard Error
    try:
        plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
        all_improvements = []
        max_steps = None
        for exp_data in all_experiment_data:
            if max_steps is None:
                max_steps = exp_data["summary"]["max_steps"]
            all_improvements.append(exp_data["summary"]["avg_improvement"])

        all_improvements = np.array(all_improvements)
        improvement_mean = np.mean(all_improvements, axis=0)
        improvement_se = np.std(all_improvements, axis=0) / np.sqrt(
            len(all_experiment_data)
        )

        colors = ["green" if v >= 0 else "red" for v in improvement_mean]
        bars = plt.bar(
            max_steps,
            improvement_mean,
            color=colors,
            alpha=0.7,
            width=40,
            label="Mean Improvement",
        )
        plt.errorbar(
            max_steps,
            improvement_mean,
            yerr=improvement_se,
            fmt="none",
            ecolor="black",
            capsize=5,
            label="Standard Error",
        )
        plt.axhline(y=0, color="black", linestyle="--", linewidth=1)
        plt.xlabel("max_steps (Simulation Length)")
        plt.ylabel("Average Improvement (%)")
        plt.title(
            f"Warehouse Robot Task Allocation\nAggregated CATA Improvement (n={len(all_experiment_data)} runs)"
        )
        plt.legend()
        plt.xticks(max_steps)
        plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3, axis="y")
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(
                working_dir, "warehouse_aggregated_improvement_by_maxsteps.png"
            ),
            dpi=150,
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating aggregated improvement bar chart: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 4: Aggregated Throughput by Agent Count with Error Bands
    try:
        plt.figure(figsize=(12, 7))
        max_steps_values = [100, 200, 300, 400, 500]
        colors = plt.cm.viridis(np.linspace(0, 1, len(max_steps_values)))

        for idx, max_steps in enumerate(max_steps_values):
            key = f"max_steps_{max_steps}"
            all_throughputs = []
            agent_counts = None
            for exp_data in all_experiment_data:
                if agent_counts is None:
                    agent_counts = np.array(
                        exp_data["hyperparam_tuning"][key]["n_agents"]
                    )
                all_throughputs.append(
                    exp_data["hyperparam_tuning"][key]["congestion_aware"]["throughput"]
                )

            all_throughputs = np.array(all_throughputs)
            throughput_mean = np.mean(all_throughputs, axis=0)
            throughput_se = np.std(all_throughputs, axis=0) / np.sqrt(
                len(all_experiment_data)
            )

            plt.errorbar(
                agent_counts,
                throughput_mean,
                yerr=throughput_se,
                fmt="o-",
                color=colors[idx],
                label=f"max_steps={max_steps} (Mean ± SE)",
                markersize=8,
                linewidth=2,
                capsize=4,
            )

        plt.xlabel("Number of Agents")
        plt.ylabel("Throughput (tasks/min)")
        plt.title(
            f"Warehouse Robot Task Allocation\nAggregated CATA Throughput by Agent Count (n={len(all_experiment_data)} runs)"
        )
        plt.legend(title="Simulation Length")
        plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(
                working_dir, "warehouse_aggregated_cata_throughput_by_agents.png"
            ),
            dpi=150,
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating aggregated throughput by agent count plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 5: Aggregated Improvement Heatmap with Mean and SE
    try:
        plt.figure(figsize=(12, 9))
        max_steps_values = [100, 200, 300, 400, 500]
        agent_counts = all_experiment_data[0]["hyperparam_tuning"]["max_steps_100"][
            "n_agents"
        ]

        all_improvement_matrices = []
        for exp_data in all_experiment_data:
            improvement_matrix = []
            for max_steps in max_steps_values:
                key = f"max_steps_{max_steps}"
                improvement_matrix.append(
                    exp_data["hyperparam_tuning"][key]["improvement"]
                )
            all_improvement_matrices.append(np.array(improvement_matrix))

        all_improvement_matrices = np.array(all_improvement_matrices)
        improvement_mean = np.mean(all_improvement_matrices, axis=0)
        improvement_se = np.std(all_improvement_matrices, axis=0) / np.sqrt(
            len(all_experiment_data)
        )

        im = plt.imshow(improvement_mean, cmap="RdYlGn", aspect="auto")
        plt.colorbar(im, label="Mean Improvement (%)")
        plt.xticks(range(len(agent_counts)), agent_counts)
        plt.yticks(range(len(max_steps_values)), max_steps_values)
        plt.xlabel("Number of Agents")
        plt.ylabel("max_steps")
        plt.title(
            f"Warehouse Robot Task Allocation\nAggregated CATA Improvement Heatmap (n={len(all_experiment_data)} runs)\nFormat: Mean ± SE"
        )

        for i in range(len(max_steps_values)):
            for j in range(len(agent_counts)):
                plt.text(
                    j,
                    i,
                    f"{improvement_mean[i, j]:.1f}%\n±{improvement_se[i, j]:.1f}",
                    ha="center",
                    va="center",
                    fontsize=8,
                )

        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "warehouse_aggregated_improvement_heatmap.png"),
            dpi=150,
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating aggregated improvement heatmap: {e}")
        plt.close()

print("All aggregated plots saved to working directory.")
